title: openpouch deploy result (instant lane success)
description: Successful `openpouch deploy [dir] --json` / MCP `openpouch_deploy`. Contract: `required` is guaranteed; results evolve ADDITIVELY. Failures use error.schema.json instead. Claim fields exist ONLY on anonymous deploys (ownership: "anonymous") — there, exactly one of `claimUrl` (default) or `claimUrlRedacted: true` (--redact-secrets / MCP default) is present. An account-owned deploy (ownership: "account", since 0.3.1) is already saved: it carries NO claim fields and its `expiresAt` rolls forward while the app is used.

url.description: THE primary result: the live, publicly shareable address.

authenticated.description: true = ran under an openpouch account key; false = anonymous free tier.

ownership.description: Who the deployment belongs to (since 0.3.1). "account" = saved to the key's account: no claim step exists and expiry rolls with use. "anonymous" = ephemeral preview with a claim link.

expiryPolicy.description: How to read `deployment.expiresAt` (since 0.3.1): "fixed" = hard deadline (anonymous/claimed); "rolling-while-used" = moves forward on every request (account-owned).

tellYourUser.description: Ready-to-relay hand-off for the HUMAN (since 0.3.3) — pass it on verbatim and unprompted: live URL + exact expiry date + every keep option (free save link / free account / paid plans from $15/month with the pricing URL) + doing-nothing-is-fine. Present only on a healthy deploy; never contains the claim link/token.

claimUrl.description: SECRET save link (carries a one-time token — treat like a password; hand it to your human PRIVATELY, never into shared reports). The shareable address is `url`, never this. Anonymous deploys only.

claimUrlRedacted.description: Present instead of claimUrl when redaction is on; the real link is stored locally in .openpouch/claim.json (0600, gitignored). Anonymous deploys only.

claimUrlIsPrivate.description: Present exactly when a claim link exists for this deploy (anonymous deploys; no longer emitted on account-owned deploys — required until 0.3.0, conditional since 0.3.1).

envVars.description: Env var NAMES set on the app — values are never echoed anywhere.

pending.description: true = container not answering yet; re-check with `openpouch verify` before sharing.

healthStatus.description: Post-deploy GET / gate: `healthy` = 200 observed; `degraded` = live but not answering 200 — do NOT share the URL, run the self-repair loop; `pending` = still starting.

healthPathCheck.description: Present when --health-path ran: the extra API-route probe (full-stack bar).

nextStep.description: Present on pending/degraded results: the exact next move.

nextCommands.description: Copy-paste follow-ups, health-path aware — no flag reconstruction needed.

dataDurability.description: Present when the app writes local data: an ephemeral preview will NOT keep it — tell your human plainly.

claimUrl.description: Omitted under redaction and on account-owned deploys.

expiresAt.description: ISO timestamp. Anonymous: hard deadline (~72h unclaimed). Account-owned: rolls forward while the app is used (see `expiryPolicy`).

app.description: L12: named-app identity, when deployed with --app.

evidence.description: The truth files written into the repo — re-reading them is always sufficient to resume.

urlStability.description: L12: whether this URL survives redeploys. 'stable' only for a named app (--app, account required): the same slug is UPDATED in place, health-checked before the swap. 'fresh-per-deploy' is the instant-lane default: every deploy mints a new URL.

app.description: L12: the named-app identity (account+app). Present only when the deploy ran with --app under an account; redeploys with the same name update the app behind the same URL.

updated.description: L12: true when this deploy UPDATED the existing named app in place (same slug/URL, content swapped after the new version passed its health gate); false on the app's first deploy.

appNotSupportedByServer.description: L12 version skew: --app was requested but the server predates named apps and ignored it — this deploy got a fresh URL and will NOT update in place.
